title: Elbaf’s Wounds Reopen
description: On pages 7–9, this part follows Unknown person #7, Rocks D. Xebec, Unknown person #8, Unknown person #9, World Government as The story examines the bloodline hatred and political violence surrounding Elbaf while the danger around Loki and the God’s Knights grows.
scene: The story examines the bloodline hatred and political violence surrounding Elbaf while the danger around Loki and the God’s Knights grows.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: What the-? It's comin' from that chest!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: only one aspect of the story was highlighted, the heroin of GARP.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It says here that the rocks pirates have been vangled!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I knew it!! Vice Admiral GARP is the strongest!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: What the hell is this article?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I can't wait to get my hands on that morgans fella!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Relax, GARP! He only wrote that stuff because the government paid him off.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The world government and the Marines are massive organizations.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: If you count the member nations' armies, we're talking about tens of millions of soldiers worldwide.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It's impossible to account for everyone's actions and motivations.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: So if the top brass ever do strike me as insane...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I'd simply turn a blind eye to it!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: You're just another promotion- chasing fool!!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Shut up!!! What good can you do barking from the bottom of the cliff?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: If you ever stray and get corrupted, I'll kill you myself!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: How reassuring! I guess that's what friends are for!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: So you're not quitting then, Garp?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: There are still things at the bottom of the cliff that need a guard dog!!!

summary: This part advances The Weight of Elbaf’s Bloodline through 38 cleaned dialogue beats, focusing on the immediate conflict, character reactions, and the larger Elbaf mystery.
